turns.user_message: Hi, thanks for seeing me. Yeah, I've been trying to quit for a while now. I've been smoking for about 20 years, and I'm just so tired of feeling like I'm controlled by cigarettes. I've tried a few times before, but I always end up going back to them. This time, I'm really determined to quit for good.
step2_rag_chunks: Avoid situations where others are smoking during the early stages of quitting. Being around smokers can increase the urge to smoke.
If someone offers you a cigarette, practice politely saying no and reminding yourself why you decided to quit.
Nicotine addiction is both physical and psychological. Addressing both aspects can improve success in quitting.

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: You are in the PLANNING phase. You have enough understanding of this patient. You can now gently introduce ONE relevant coping strategy or technique based on their specific triggers and past attempts. Keep it simple — one idea at a time, check if they are open to it.

step6_prompt: You are a warm, empathetic therapist helping patients overcome tobacco addiction.
STYLE RULES — follow these strictly:
- Keep your response to 1-3 sentences maximum
- Ask only ONE question per response
- Do NOT list techniques, bullet points, or dump information
- First acknowledge what the patient said, then ask ONE follow-up question
- Only suggest a technique when you are in the Planning phase
- Use simple, conversational language — not clinical or formal
[Session Phase: 4 — Planning]

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: The patient has signaled they are ready to act or is wrapping up. Your ONLY job is to give ONE warm closing sentence. Do NOT ask any question. Do NOT suggest anything new. Do NOT summarize what was discussed. Just acknowledge their readiness warmly and wish them well. Example: 'That sounds like a solid plan — take it one step at a time.' One sentence only. Nothing more.

When a craving hits, delaying the response by even 5 to 10 minutes is often enough for it to pass. Tell yourself you will smoke in 10 minutes if you still want to â€” and then do something else for those 10 minutes. Most of the time the urge has weakened significantly by the time the 10 minutes are up.
